title: [Bug]: can't pull and image as the underneath WSL can't access internet
body:
### Issue Description

 can't modify  /etc/resolv.conf

```
# This file was automatically generated by WSL. To stop automatic generation of this file, add the following entry to /etc/wsl.conf:
# [network]
# generateResolvConf = false
nameserver 192.168.128.1
```

even updated, after save, it is immediately rollback




### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  update /etc/resolv.conf
2.  cat /etc/resolv.conf , still the old content
3.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
/etc/resolv.conf can be overwrite, so nameserver 8.8.8.8

comments.body: @n1hility This looks like a WSL2 issue, not a Podman one - would you agree?

That's right. This file is regenerated by WSL, if you want to override it, automatic generation needs to be disabled as specified in the comments in the file.

We have a feature request to make this simpler to specify when needed: #15344
Here is a link with more info on disabling auto-gen: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/wsl-config

@n1hility,  when will the simplified feature release ?

I did tried, both not works for me
`1.  podman machine init --image-path  "..\..\podman\rootfs.tar.xz"    `
     # with updated "etc\wsl.conf" in xz file
`2. can't find a way to update \\wsl.localhost\podman-machine-default\etc\wsl.conf`
    # "please check whether if the file is opened in another program"

@byunru does the following work:

1. `podman machine stop`
2.  `wsl --shutdown`
3.  `wsl -d podman-machine-default`
4.  `vi /etc/wsl.conf` # (make and save changes adding generateResolveConf)
5.  `vi /etc/resolv.conf`
6.  `exit` back to powershell or cmd
7.  `wsl --shutdown`
8.  `wsl -d podman-machine-default cat /etc/resolv.conf` # verify looks correct

If it's still wrong you might need to do a wsl --update to pull in a more recent version, there was some known bugs with the setting.

still not work, I'll wait for the new release.
@n1hility, thanks

lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root     39 Jan 16 12:01 resolv.conf -> ../run/systemd/resolve/stub-resolv.conf
